verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is synthesized as a type I transmembrane proform (pro-EREG) with its EGF-like ectodomain exposed on the outer face of the plasma membrane. This membrane-anchored precursor is itself surface-accessible and capable of juxtacrine signaling before ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding releases the soluble mature form. The proform thus presents its protein body on the extracellular leaflet via its own TM anchor. However, the dominant described biology is shedding to a soluble ligand, making this contextual (membrane-proform state) rather than a constitutively stable surface protein.
